---
title: "Huang's Law and the Democratization of NLP"
description: "If GPU performance keeps doubling, training GPT-3 could fall to ~$15k on a single GPU within eight years."
author: "synesis"
date: "2020-09-20"
categories: [NLP, GPT-3, deep learning, links]
image: cover.jpg
---

![Huang's Law. Image from [WSJ](https://www.wsj.com/articles/huangs-law-is-the-new-moores-law-and-explains-why-nvidia-wants-arm-11600488001).](cover.jpg){fig-align="center"}

Huang's Law: <https://lnkd.in/gVZjYWg>

Today GPT-3 (175b parameters) takes a single V100 355 years, or $4.6m, to train. In 8 years of time we're looking at a bit over 1 year, or ~$15k to train, on a single GPU.

With massive parallelism this would place models of hundreds of billions of parameters in a very affordable range.

What does this say about NLP? Democratization of NLP?


*Originally posted on [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/benjaminhan_nlp-activity-6713267126339280896-GRvs).*
